title: The lambda parameter should be preferred instead of capturing arguments in ConcurrentDictionary methods
description: The lambda parameter should be used instead of capturing arguments in "ConcurrentDictionary" methodsAnalyze your codeintentionality-efficientmaintainabilityCode SmellperformanceWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoWhen using theConcurrentDictionary, there are many overloads of theGetOrAddandAddOrUpdatemethods that take both aTKeyargument and a lambda that expects aTKeyparameter. This means that the right side of the lambda can be written using either the lambda’s parameter or the method’s argument. However, using the method’s argument leads to the lambda capturing it, and the compiler will need to generate a class and instantiate it before the call.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

noncompliant: dict.AddOrUpdate(key, k => key.ToString(), (k, v) => key.ToString()); // Noncompliant - captures 'key' argument
compliant: dict.AddOrUpdate(key, k => k.ToString(), (k, v) => k.ToString()); // Compliant - uses lambda parameter 'k'

checks.message: In ConcurrentDictionary GetOrAdd/AddOrUpdate, use the lambda parameter instead of capturing the method argument to avoid unnecessary allocations

logic.detection_conditions: ConcurrentDictionary.GetOrAdd or AddOrUpdate call where lambda captures the key argument instead of using lambda parameter
logic.triggering_conditions: source contains GetOrAdd/AddOrUpdate call pattern where the captured variable matches the method argument
never_trigger_on: Lambda that uses the lambda parameter (k => k.ToString())
logic.reasoning: When using GetOrAdd or AddOrUpdate with a lambda, capturing the method's key argument causes the compiler to generate a closure class. Using the lambda parameter instead avoids this allocation overhead.
